name: elastalert2

metadata.name: elastalert2
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License

documentation:
# ElastAlert 2

ElastAlert 2 is a standalone software tool for alerting on anomalies, spikes, or other patterns of interest from data in [Elasticsearch][10] and [OpenSearch][9].

ElastAlert 2 is backwards compatible with the original [ElastAlert][0] rules.

![CI Workflow](https://github.com/jertel/elastalert/workflows/master_build_test/badge.svg)

## Docker and Kubernetes

ElastAlert 2 is well-suited to being run as a microservice, and is available
as an image on [Docker Hub][2] and on [GitHub Container Registry][11]. For more instructions on how to
configure and run ElastAlert 2 using Docker, see [here][8].

A [Helm chart][7] is also included for easy configuration as a Kubernetes deployment. 

## Documentation

Documentation, including an FAQ, for ElastAlert 2 can be found on [readthedocs.com][3]. This is the place to start if you're not familiar with ElastAlert 2 at all.

Elasticsearch 8 support is documented in the [FAQ][12].

The full list of platforms that ElastAlert 2 can fire alerts into can be found [in the documentation][4].

## Contributing

Please see our [contributing guidelines][6].

## Security

See our [security policy][13] for reporting urgent vulnerabilities.

## License

ElastAlert 2 is licensed under the [Apache License, Version 2.0][5].

[0]: https://github.com/yelp/elastalert
[1]: https://github.com/jertel/elastalert2/blob/master/examples/config.yaml.example
[2]: https://hub.docker.com/r/jertel/elastalert2
[3]: https://elastalert2.readthedocs.io/
[4]: https://elastalert2.readthedocs.io/en/latest/alerts.html#alert-types
[5]: https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
[6]: https://github.com/jertel/elastalert2/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md
[7]: https://github.com/jertel/elastalert2/tree/master/chart/elastalert2
[8]: https://elastalert2.readthedocs.io/en/latest/running_elastalert.html
[9]: https://opensearch.org/
[10]: https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch
[11]: https://github.com/jertel/elastalert2/pkgs/container/elastalert2%2Felastalert2
[12]: https://elastalert2.readthedocs.io/en/latest/recipes/faq.html#does-elastalert-2-support-elasticsearch-8
[13]: https://github.com/jertel/elastalert2/blob/master/SECURITY.md

functions.name: tests.alerters.iris_test.test_iris_make_alert_auto_description
documentation:
Test for the built-in elastalert2 create_title and create_body functions

These functions use the alert_subject and alert_text fields to automatically
build the title and description based on alert match data if available.

functions.name: tests.alerters.iris_test.test_iris_make_alert_auto_description_args
documentation:
Test for the built-in elastalert2 create_title and create_body functions

These functions use the alert_subject and alert_text fields to automatically
build the title and description based on alert match data if available.

This test specifically uses _args for the alert_text to ensure match args
are passed through properly.

methods.name: elastalert.enhancements.BaseEnhancement.process
documentation: Modify the contents of match, a dictionary, in some way

Enhancements take a match dictionary object and modify it in some way to
enhance an alert. These are specified in each rule under the match_enhancements option.
Generally, the key value pairs in the match module will be contained in the alert body.

modules.name: elastalert.util
documentation: -*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: elastalert.util.get_module
documentation:
Loads a module and returns a specific object.
module_name should 'module.file.object'.
Returns object or raises EAException on error.

functions.name: elastalert.util.new_get_event_ts
documentation:
Constructs a lambda that may be called to extract the timestamp field
from a given event.

:returns: A callable function that takes an event and outputs that event's
timestamp field.

functions.name: elastalert.util._find_es_dict_by_key
documentation:
Performs a divide-and-conquer recursive search to resolve a term string
string as compatible dictionary key and list index combination.  It attempts
to resolve the ambiguity for . and "keyword" being either literals or delimiters.

For example
   'my.dotted.name.a_child_field.somelist[4]'
may be found as
    lookup_dict['my.dotted.name']['a_child_field']['somelist'][4]
or found as
    lookup_dict['my']['dotted.name']['a_child_field.somelist'][4]

1. Prefers longer fieldname matches
2. No wildcards exist within the provided ES search terms (these are treated as string literals)
3. Firstly assumes 'keyword' is a fieldname, then assumes 'keyword' is a subfield specifier for a multifield

functions.name: elastalert.util.set_es_key
documentation:
Looks up the location that the term maps to and sets it to the given value.
:returns: True if the value was set successfully, False otherwise.

functions.name: elastalert.util.lookup_es_key
documentation:
Performs iterative dictionary search for the given term.
:returns: The value identified by term or None if it cannot be found.

functions.name: elastalert.util.ts_utc_to_tz
documentation: Convert utc time to local time.

functions.name: elastalert.util.inc_ts
documentation: Increment a timestamp by milliseconds.

functions.name: elastalert.util.pretty_ts
documentation:
Pretty-format the given timestamp (to be printed or logged hereafter).
If tz, the timestamp will be converted to local time.
Format: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM TZ

functions.name: elastalert.util.ts_add
documentation: Allows a timedelta (td) add operation on a string timestamp (ts)

functions.name: elastalert.util.hashable
documentation:
Convert obj to a hashable obj.
We use the value of some fields from Elasticsearch as keys for dictionaries. This means
that whatever Elasticsearch returns must be hashable, and it sometimes returns a list or dict.

functions.name: elastalert.util.format_index
documentation:
Takes an index, specified using strftime format, start and end time timestamps,
and outputs a wildcard based index string to match all possible timestamps.

functions.name: elastalert.util.cronite_datetime_to_timestamp
documentation: Converts a `datetime` object `d` into a UNIX timestamp.

functions.name: elastalert.util.replace_dots_in_field_names
documentation:
This method destructively modifies document by replacing any dots in
field names with an underscore.

functions.name: elastalert.util.elasticsearch_client
documentation: returns an {class}`ElasticSearchClient` instance configured using an es_conn_config

functions.name: elastalert.util.build_es_conn_config
documentation:
Given a conf dictionary w/ raw config properties 'use_ssl', 'es_host', 'es_port'
'es_username' and 'es_password', this will return a new dictionary
with properly initialized values for 'es_host', 'es_port', 'use_ssl' and 'http_auth' which
will be a basicauth username:password formatted string

functions.name: elastalert.util.parse_duration
documentation: Convert `unit=num` spec into a `timedelta` object.

functions.name: elastalert.util.parse_deadline
documentation: Convert `unit=num` spec into a `datetime` object.

functions.name: elastalert.util.resolve_string
documentation:
Given a python string that may contain references to fields on the match dictionary,

: the strings are replaced using the corresponding values.

However, if the referenced field is not found on the dictionary,

: it is replaced by a default string.

Strings can be formatted using the old-style format ('%(field)s') or

: the new-style format ('{match[field]}').

```
param string

A string that may contain references to values of the 'match' dictionary.

param match

A dictionary with the values to replace where referenced by keys in the string.

param missing_text

The default text to replace a formatter with if the field doesnt exist.
```

functions.name: elastalert.util.should_scrolling_continue
documentation:
Tells about a rule config if it can scroll still or should stop the scrolling.

```
param

rule_conf as dict

rtype

bool
```

functions.name: elastalert.util._expand_string_into_dict
documentation:
Converts a encapsulated string-dict to a sequence of dict. Use separator (default '.') to split the string.
Example:

> string1.string2.stringN : value -> {string1: {string2: {string3: value}}

```
param string

The encapsulated "string-dict"

param value

Value associated to the last field of the "string-dict"

param sep

Separator character. Default: '.'

rtype

dict
```

functions.name: elastalert.util.expand_string_into_dict
documentation:
Useful function to "compile" a string-dict string used in metric and percentage rules into a dictionary sequence.

```
param dictionary

The dictionary dict

param string

String Key

param value

String Value

param sep

Separator character. Default: '.'

rtype

dict
```

functions.name: elastalert.util.format_string
documentation:
Formats number, supporting %-format and str.format() syntax.

```
param format_config

string format syntax, for example '{:.2%}' or '%.2f'

param target_value

number to format

rtype

string
```

functions.name: elastalert.util.parse_hosts
documentation:
Convert host str like "host1:port1, host2:port2" to list
\:param host str: hostnames (separated with comma ) or single host name
\:param port: default to 9200
\:return: list of hosts

modules.name: elastalert.ruletypes
documentation: -*- coding: utf-8 -*-

classes.name: elastalert.ruletypes.RuleType

methods.name: elastalert.ruletypes.RuleType.add_data
documentation:
The function that the ElastAlert client calls with results from ES.
Data is a list of dictionaries, from Elasticsearch.

```
param data

A list of events, each of which is a dictionary of terms.
```

methods.name: elastalert.ruletypes.RuleType.add_match
documentation:
This function is called on all matching events. Rules use it to add
extra information about the context of a match. Event is a dictionary
containing terms directly from Elasticsearch and alerts will report
all of the information.

```
param event

The matching event, a dictionary of terms.
```

methods.name: elastalert.ruletypes.RuleType.get_match_str
documentation:
Returns a string that gives more context about a match.

```
param match

The matching event, a dictionary of terms.

return

A user facing string describing the match.
```

methods.name: elastalert.ruletypes.RuleType.garbage_collect
documentation:
Gets called periodically to remove old data that is useless beyond given timestamp.
May also be used to compute things in the absence of new data.

```
param timestamp

A timestamp indicating the rule has been run up to that point.
```

methods.name: elastalert.ruletypes.RuleType.add_count_data
documentation:
Gets called when a rule has use_count_query set to True. Called to add data from querying to the rule.

```
param counts

A dictionary mapping timestamps to hit counts.
```

methods.name: elastalert.ruletypes.RuleType.add_terms_data
documentation:
Gets called when a rule has use_terms_query set to True.

```
param terms

A list of buckets with a key, corresponding to query_key, and the count
```

methods.name: elastalert.ruletypes.RuleType.add_aggregation_data
documentation:
Gets called when a rule has use_terms_query set to True.
\:param terms: A list of buckets with a key, corresponding to query_key, and the count

The base class for a rule type.
The class must implement add_data and add any matches to self.matches.

```
param rules

A rule configuration.
```

classes.name: elastalert.ruletypes.CompareRule

methods.name: elastalert.ruletypes.CompareRule.expand_entries

Expand entries specified in files using the '!file' directive, if there are
any, then add everything to a set.

documentation: An event is a match if this returns true
A base class for matching a specific term by passing it to a compare function

classes.name: elastalert.ruletypes.BlacklistRule

classes.name: elastalert.ruletypes.WhitelistRule

classes.name: elastalert.ruletypes.ChangeRule
documentation: A rule that will store values for a certain term and match if those values change

classes.name: elastalert.ruletypes.AnyRule

classes.name: elastalert.ruletypes.EventWindow

documentation:
Add an event to the window. Event should be of the form (dict, count).
This will also pop the oldest events and call onRemoved on them until the
window size is less than timeframe.

methods.name: elastalert.ruletypes.EventWindow.duration
documentation: Get the size in timedelta of the window.

methods.name: elastalert.ruletypes.EventWindow.count
documentation: Count the number of events in the window.

documentation: Compute the mean of the value_field in the window.

methods.name: elastalert.ruletypes.EventWindow.min
documentation: The minimum of the value_field in the window.

methods.name: elastalert.ruletypes.EventWindow.max
documentation: The maximum of the value_field in the window.

methods.name: elastalert.ruletypes.EventWindow.append_middle
documentation:
Attempt to place the event in the correct location in our deque.
Returns True if successful, otherwise False.
A container for hold event counts for rules which need a chronological ordered event window.

A rule that uses two sliding windows to compare relative event frequency.

classes.name: elastalert.ruletypes.SpikeMetricAggregationRule

methods.name: elastalert.ruletypes.SpikeMetricAggregationRule.generate_aggregation_query
documentation: Lifted from MetricAggregationRule

methods.name: elastalert.ruletypes.SpikeMetricAggregationRule.add_aggregation_data
documentation:
BaseAggregationRule.add_aggregation_data unpacks our results and runs checks directly against hardcoded cutoffs.
We instead want to use all of our SpikeRule.handle_event inherited logic (current/reference) from
the aggregation's "value" key to determine spikes from aggregations

methods.name: elastalert.ruletypes.SpikeMetricAggregationRule.unwrap_term_buckets
documentation:
create separate spike event trackers for each term,
handle compound query keys

methods.name: elastalert.ruletypes.SpikeMetricAggregationRule.get_match_str
documentation: Overwrite SpikeRule's message to relate to the aggregation type & field instead of count
A rule that matches when there is a spike in an aggregated event compared to its reference point

modules.name: elastalert.elastalert
documentation: -*- coding: utf-8 -*-

classes.name: elastalert.elastalert.ElastAlerter

methods.name: elastalert.elastalert.ElastAlerter.get_index
documentation:
Gets the index for a rule. If strftime is set and starttime and endtime
are provided, it will return a comma seperated list of indices. If strftime
is set but starttime and endtime are not provided, it will replace all format
tokens with a wildcard.

methods.name: elastalert.elastalert.ElastAlerter.get_query
documentation:
Returns a query dict that will apply a list of filters, filter by
start and end time, and sort results by timestamp.

```
param filters

A list of Elasticsearch filters to use.

param starttime

A timestamp to use as the start time of the query.

param endtime

A timestamp to use as the end time of the query.

param sort

If true, sort results by timestamp. (Default True)

return

A query dictionary to pass to Elasticsearch.
```

methods.name: elastalert.elastalert.ElastAlerter.get_terms_query
documentation: Takes a query generated by get_query and outputs a aggregation query

methods.name: elastalert.elastalert.ElastAlerter.get_aggregation_query
documentation: Takes a query generated by get_query and outputs a aggregation query

methods.name: elastalert.elastalert.ElastAlerter.get_index_start
documentation:
Query for one result sorted by timestamp to find the beginning of the index.

```
param index

The index of which to find the earliest event.

return

Timestamp of the earliest event.
```

methods.name: elastalert.elastalert.ElastAlerter.process_hits
documentation:
Update the \_source field for each hit received from ES based on the rule configuration.

This replaces timestamps with datetime objects,
folds important fields into \_source and creates compound query_keys.

```
return

A list of processed _source dictionaries.
```

methods.name: elastalert.elastalert.ElastAlerter.get_hits
documentation:
Query Elasticsearch for the given rule and return the results.
\:param rule: The rule configuration.
\:param starttime: The earliest time to query.
\:param endtime: The latest time to query.
\:return: A list of hits, bounded by rule['max_query_size'] (or self.max_query_size).

methods.name: elastalert.elastalert.ElastAlerter.get_hits_count
documentation:
Query Elasticsearch for the count of results and returns a list of timestamps
equal to the endtime. This allows the results to be passed to rules which expect
an object for each hit.

```
param rule

The rule configuration dictionary.

param starttime

The earliest time to query.

param endtime

The latest time to query.

return

A dictionary mapping timestamps to number of hits for that time period.
```

methods.name: elastalert.elastalert.ElastAlerter.run_query
documentation:
Query for the rule and pass all of the results to the RuleType instance.

```
param rule

The rule configuration.

param start

The earliest time to query.

param end

The latest time to query.
```

Returns True on success and False on failure.

methods.name: elastalert.elastalert.ElastAlerter.get_starttime
documentation:
Query ES for the last time we ran this rule.

```
param rule

The rule configuration.

return

A timestamp or None.
```

methods.name: elastalert.elastalert.ElastAlerter.set_starttime
documentation: Given a rule and an endtime, sets the appropriate starttime for it.

methods.name: elastalert.elastalert.ElastAlerter.get_segment_size
documentation:
The segment size is either buffer_size for queries which can overlap or run_every for queries
which must be strictly separate. This mimicks the query size for when ElastAlert is running continuously.

methods.name: elastalert.elastalert.ElastAlerter.enhance_filter
documentation:
If there is a blacklist or whitelist in rule then we add it to the filter.
It adds it as a query_string. If there is already an query string its is appended
with blacklist or whitelist.

```
param rule



return

```

methods.name: elastalert.elastalert.ElastAlerter.run_rule

Run a rule for a given time period, including querying and alerting on results.

```
The rule configuration.

param starttime

The earliest timestamp to query.

param endtime

The latest timestamp to query.

return

The number of matches that the rule produced.
```

controls when queries are run, and passes information between rules and alerts.

```
An argparse arguments instance. Should contain debug and start
```
